# K8s Pod OOMKilled

## When to Use
Use this when a Kubernetes pod is OOMKilled after exceeding its memory limit.

## Symptoms
Pod restart count increases and the last terminated reason is OOMKilled.

## Fix
- Check the last terminated reason, exit code, and memory limit before proposing remediation.
- Record owner approval before changing resource limits through the owning deployment workflow.
- Update the workload memory limit only through the approved deployment path, then watch restart count and memory headroom.

## Verification
Confirm restart count stops increasing and memory headroom remains healthy.

## Reusable Lessons
Do not delete production pods automatically; preserve the incident evidence and verify owner-approved mitigation.
